title: T-059 — PRD marker discipline sweep (87 unmarked headings in 00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md)
prompt:
# T-059 — PRD marker discipline sweep

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §1 (six principles), §2 (marker discipline)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Audit source: `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-wave-a-carry-forward-backlog.md` CF-25
Severity: P2 (pre-existing debt; no functional impact; required before marker-discipline ADR-035 compliance is claimable)

## Goal
Add the correct marker (one of `[UNIVERSAL]`, `[APEX-CONFIG]`, `[EVOLVING]`, `[LEGACY-D365]`) to all 87 currently-unmarked headings in `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md`, per ADR-035 marker discipline. Update `scripts/check-markers.mjs` allowlist if any heading legitimately should remain unmarked (e.g., front-matter, table-of-contents).

## Implementation contract
Run `node scripts/check-markers.mjs docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` to enumerate the 87 unmarked headings. For each one:
1. Read the section's content carefully.
2. Choose the correct marker per ADR-035:
   - `[UNIVERSAL]` — applies to all tenants/orgs unconditionally.
   - `[APEX-CONFIG]` — Apex-specific configuration; modifiable per tenant.
   - `[EVOLVING]` — placeholder/active design that will change in later waves.
   - `[LEGACY-D365]` — D365 import/export compatibility only; not a forward contract.
3. Append the marker in square brackets after the heading text: `## §X.Y Heading [MARKER]`.

Do NOT modify the section bodies. Do NOT renumber headings. Do NOT introduce new markers beyond the four ADR-035 set.

If any heading genuinely cannot be classified (e.g., it is the document title or a TOC entry), add it to `scripts/check-markers.mjs` allowlist with a one-line justification comment.

## Files (scope)
- docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md [modify — append markers to 87 headings]
- scripts/check-markers.mjs [modify if allowlist needs new entries]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the marker sweep, when check-markers.mjs runs over 00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md, then it exits 0.
2. Given the chosen markers, when each heading is reviewed, then it carries exactly one of the four ADR-035 markers; AND Given unchanged bodies, when git diff --stat runs, then only heading lines are modified.
3. Given allowlist updates, when check-markers.mjs runs over other 14 PRDs, then it still exits 0.
4. Given the merged result, when pnpm --filter web build runs, then it succeeds.
## Test strategy
- RED: capture the current count of unmarked headings via `node scripts/check-markers.mjs docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md | wc -l` to confirm 87 baseline.
- After GREEN, re-run the same command and confirm exit 0.
- `git diff --stat` to confirm only heading-line changes.
- Run `node scripts/check-markers.mjs` over each of the 15 module PRDs to confirm no regression.

## Risk red lines
- Do NOT modify section bodies.
- Do NOT renumber headings.
- Do NOT introduce new markers beyond the four ADR-035 set.
- Do NOT mass-mark everything `[UNIVERSAL]` — each heading deserves a thoughtful classification.
- Do NOT modify other PRD files.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the writing-plans skill (group headings by section before classifying) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
